decision_summary: Constraints are satisfied with a margin of ~16°C. Since geometry changes are hitting legality limits and recent improvements have stalled, the strategy shifts to material tuning. Increasing the heat spreader conductivity is the highest priority lever to further reduce the chip peak temperature by enhancing lateral heat spreading.

changes.reason: Maximizing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution, reducing the local hot spot intensity on the chip. This is the primary recommended lever when geometry adjustments are constrained.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns: doubling conductivity from an already high value (500) may yield smaller absolute temperature drops than previous steps.
Physical realism: 1000 W/(m*K) exceeds standard copper but represents advanced composites or theoretical limits for this teaching case.
